mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly supporting higher equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter through earnings revisions.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase MoM
failure_condition: Joint positive growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-stat > 1.96 over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which resolves cost pressures and supports positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in >60% of observations during the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals accelerating inflation, prompting market pricing of imminent monetary tightening and generating negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in >70% of high-inflation trigger months despite the tightening signal.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly nonfarm payroll additions above 300,000 reflect robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings growth over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between PAYEMS MoM changes and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high real GDP growth and elevated CPI levels create a stagflationary risk profile, forcing central banks to prioritize inflation control over growth, which compresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of instances where both GDP and CPI thresholds are simultaneously met.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals acute manufacturing contraction and supply chain bottlenecks, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in >65% of triggered decline instances despite the manufacturing contraction signal.
